OCP.description: World's largest phosphate exporter, state-controlled. Produces phosphate rock, phosphoric acid, and DAP/MAP fertilizers sold globally.

macro_sensitivity.dirham_weakness: positive — export revenues are USD-denominated, convert to more MAD
macro_sensitivity.oil_rise: negative — energy-intensive production raises operating costs
macro_sensitivity.global_recession: negative — fertilizer demand falls with reduced agricultural investment
macro_sensitivity.eur_mad_weakness: neutral — most OCP exports priced in USD not EUR

ATW.description: Attijariwafa Bank — largest bank in Morocco and one of the largest in Africa. Operations in 25+ African countries. Retail, corporate, and investment banking.

macro_sensitivity.rate_hike: negative short-term (cost of funds rises, existing loan margins compress)
macro_sensitivity.dirham_weakness: mixed — African subsidiary revenues lose value when converted to MAD
macro_sensitivity.gdp_growth: positive — strong economy drives credit demand

BCP.description: Banque Centrale Populaire — second-largest banking group in Morocco. Strong retail banking network, significant government ties, large SME lending book.

macro_sensitivity.rate_hike: negative — raises funding costs, compresses retail loan margins
macro_sensitivity.gdp_growth: positive — SME activity drives loan demand

BOA.description: Bank of Africa (formerly BMCE Bank) — major Moroccan bank with pan-African presence across 20+ countries. Strong in trade finance and correspondent banking.
risks: African political risk in operating markets
trade finance exposure to commodity exporters

macro_sensitivity.commodity_boom: positive — drives trade finance volumes for African commodity exporters

IAM.description: Maroc Telecom (Itissalat Al-Maghrib) — dominant telecom operator in Morocco, majority owned by UAE's e& (formerly Etisalat). Fixed, mobile, internet, and African subsidiaries.
risks: competition from Orange Maroc and InwiMaroc

macro_sensitivity.rate_hike: low — telecom is defensive, low debt sensitivity
macro_sensitivity.gdp_growth: low-positive — defensive stock, weak correlation to economic cycle
macro_sensitivity.regulatory_risk: high — tariff changes directly affect revenue

MNG.description: Managem — Moroccan mining conglomerate producing copper, gold, cobalt, silver, and zinc across Africa. Majority owned by SNI (ONA group).

macro_sensitivity.copper_rise: strongly positive — copper is the largest revenue contributor
macro_sensitivity.cobalt_rise: positive — EV battery supply chain demand
macro_sensitivity.oil_rise: negative — raises energy costs at mine sites

LHM.description: LafargeHolcim Maroc — largest cement producer in Morocco, part of the global Holcim group. Serves construction, infrastructure, and housing sectors.

macro_sensitivity.gdp_growth: positive — construction tracks economic growth
macro_sensitivity.rate_hike: negative — raises mortgage costs, slows housing
macro_sensitivity.government_spend: positive — infrastructure projects drive bulk cement demand

CIMAR.description: Ciments du Maroc — second-largest cement producer in Morocco, part of the HeidelbergMaterials group. Strong in southern Morocco and export markets.

macro_sensitivity.gdp_growth: positive — construction tracks GDP

CSUMAR.description: Cosumar — Morocco's monopoly sugar refiner and distributor. Refines imported raw sugar and processes domestic sugar beet and cane. Government-regulated consumer prices.
risks: global raw sugar price spikes raise input costs

macro_sensitivity.sugar_price_rise: negative — raises input costs if not passed to consumers via regulated prices
macro_sensitivity.subsidy_reform: negative — government reducing sugar subsidies compresses margins
macro_sensitivity.gdp_growth: low sensitivity — sugar is a staple commodity

TQM.description: TotalEnergies Marketing Maroc — fuel distribution and petroleum product marketing across Morocco. Petrol stations, lubricants, aviation fuel, and LPG.
key_drivers: EUR/MAD and USD/MAD rate (products priced in USD)
risks: competition from Vivo Energy / Shell Maroc

macro_sensitivity.oil_rise: mixed — higher prices raise costs but also inventory gains
macro_sensitivity.dirham_weakness: negative — imported products cost more in MAD
macro_sensitivity.gdp_growth: positive — economic activity drives fuel consumption

ADH.description: Addoha — one of Morocco's largest residential real estate developers. Focus on affordable and mid-range housing. Operations across Morocco and some African markets.

macro_sensitivity.rate_hike: strongly negative — mortgage affordability falls sharply
macro_sensitivity.rate_cut: strongly positive — unlocks latent housing demand
macro_sensitivity.government_housing_policy: high impact — subsidized housing programs are core revenue

ADI.description: Alliances Développement Immobilier — real estate developer focused on mid-to-high-end residential, tourism real estate, and social housing contracts.

macro_sensitivity.tourism_recovery: positive — drives resort and second-home demand

LBV.description: Label'Vie — largest supermarket and hypermarket operator in Morocco. Carrefour franchisee. Consumer staples and non-food retail across Morocco.
risks: competition from traditional trade and e-commerce

macro_sensitivity.inflation: mixed — higher food prices boost revenue but squeeze margins on non-food
macro_sensitivity.gdp_growth: positive — consumer spending rises with income
macro_sensitivity.dirham_weakness: negative — imported goods cost more, pressure on margins

WAA.description: Wafa Assurance — leading Moroccan insurer (part of Attijariwafa group). Life insurance, property and casualty, and health insurance.
risks: investment portfolio sensitivity to bond yields

macro_sensitivity.rate_rise: positive — higher bond yields boost investment portfolio income
macro_sensitivity.gdp_growth: positive — higher incomes drive insurance adoption

ATL.description: Atlanta — mid-sized Moroccan insurance company offering property, casualty, life, and transport insurance.

macro_sensitivity.gdp_growth: positive — drives commercial insurance demand

MSA.description: Marsa Maroc — state-controlled port operator managing container terminals and bulk cargo facilities at Morocco's major ports (Casablanca, Agadir, Nador, Tanger).

macro_sensitivity.global_trade_growth: positive — higher trade flows increase throughput
macro_sensitivity.oil_price: indirect positive — Morocco's phosphate exports are energy-linked
macro_sensitivity.dirham_weakness: positive — port fees partly USD/EUR denominated

RISMA.description: Risma — Morocco's largest hotel operator, managing Accor-branded hotels (Ibis, Novotel, Mercure, Sofitel) across Morocco.
risks: competition from independent hotels and Airbnb

macro_sensitivity.eur_mad_rise: positive — European tourists spend more in MAD terms
macro_sensitivity.oil_rise: negative — raises European airfare, reduces tourist arrivals

HPS.description: HPS (Hightech Payment Systems) — Moroccan fintech providing payment processing software and services to banks and financial institutions globally. Niche B2B player.
key_drivers: software license and SaaS revenue growth

macro_sensitivity.gdp_growth: low direct sensitivity — B2B software revenues are contractual
macro_sensitivity.dirham_weakness: positive — significant USD/EUR revenue from international clients
macro_sensitivity.fintech_adoption: positive — more digital payments drive transaction-based revenue

CIH.description: CIH Bank (Crédit Immobilier et Hôtelier) — specialised Moroccan bank historically focused on real estate and hospitality financing, now a broader retail bank.
risks: rate hike impact on mortgage book

macro_sensitivity.rate_hike: negative — mortgage affordability falls, new lending slows

CDM.description: Crédit du Maroc — subsidiary of Crédit Agricole (France). Retail and corporate banking in Morocco with strong agricultural lending heritage.

macro_sensitivity.rainfall_harvest: positive — good harvest years boost agricultural loan repayment

BMCI.description: BMCI — BNP Paribas subsidiary in Morocco. Corporate and retail banking with strong ties to French corporate clients operating in Morocco.

macro_sensitivity.france_morocco_trade: positive — French corporate activity in Morocco drives revenues

SON.description: Sonasid — Morocco's leading flat steel producer (part of ArcelorMittal group). Supplies construction and industrial sectors with rebar, wire rod, and billets.
risks: import competition from cheap Chinese steel

macro_sensitivity.electricity_cost: high sensitivity — electric arc furnace is energy-intensive

AKDITAL.description: Akdital Holding — Morocco's largest private hospital network, IPO'd on the BVC in 2022. Operates clinics and specialized hospitals across Morocco, with an aggressive expansion plan targeting 50+ facilities.
key_drivers: Morocco's AMO universal health coverage rollout (driving private clinic demand)
expansion capex and new clinic openings
risks: regulatory tariff caps on medical acts
high capex burden from rapid expansion

macro_sensitivity.amo_expansion: strongly positive — generalization of Morocco's mandatory health insurance (AMO) channels millions of previously uninsured patients to private clinics
macro_sensitivity.gdp_growth: positive — higher incomes drive demand for private healthcare over public system
macro_sensitivity.rate_hike: negative — high capex expansion financed partly with debt; rising cost of capital slows new clinic openings
macro_sensitivity.dirham_weakness: negative — medical equipment is imported, costs rise in MAD

TAQA.description: Taqa Morocco — largest private power producer in Morocco. Operates the Jorf Lasfar coal-fired power complex (2,056 MW capacity) under a long-term Power Purchase Agreement (PPA) with ONEE (Office National de l'Electricité). Majority owned by Abu Dhabi's TAQA group.
risks: coal price spikes raise production costs above PPA compensation mechanism
ONEE payment delays or credit risk

macro_sensitivity.coal_price_rise: negative — fuel cost rises; PPA partially compensates but with lags
macro_sensitivity.dirham_weakness: negative — coal imports are USD-denominated, raising MAD costs
macro_sensitivity.oil_rise: indirect negative — higher energy costs broadly
macro_sensitivity.onee_credit_risk: high — ONEE is the sole off-taker; ONEE financial stress directly threatens revenue
macro_sensitivity.energy_transition: long-term negative — regulatory pressure to exit coal by 2030-2035

AUTO.description: Auto Hall — Morocco's largest automobile distributor, listed on BVC. Distributes Ford passenger cars, Iveco commercial vehicles, Mitsubishi Fuso trucks, and Manitou material handling equipment.
key_drivers: credit availability for auto loans (Bank Al-Maghrib rate)
risks: interest rate hikes reduce auto loan affordability
EV transition disrupting traditional distribution model
supply chain disruptions affecting vehicle availability

macro_sensitivity.rate_hike: negative — auto loans become more expensive, dampening sales
macro_sensitivity.gdp_growth: strongly positive — vehicle sales are highly correlated to consumer confidence and income
macro_sensitivity.oil_rise: mixed — raises fuel costs for customers, may shift demand toward smaller/more efficient vehicles
macro_sensitivity.dirham_weakness: negative — imported vehicles priced in EUR/USD become more expensive in MAD
macro_sensitivity.world_cup_2030: positive — large vehicle fleet purchases for transport logistics and tourism

MUT.description: Mutandis — Moroccan diversified consumer goods group. Portfolio includes cleaning and hygiene products, mineral water (Sotherma brand), canned fish and seafood, and personal care products sold across Morocco and exported to Africa.
key_drivers: product pricing power vs. commodity input costs

macro_sensitivity.inflation: mixed — raises input costs but also justifies price increases on staple products
macro_sensitivity.gdp_growth: positive — higher incomes lift FMCG consumption
macro_sensitivity.oil_rise: negative — raises packaging (plastic resin) and transport costs
macro_sensitivity.dirham_weakness: mixed — some imported inputs cost more, but export revenues worth more in MAD
macro_sensitivity.africa_growth: positive — expanding middle class in West Africa drives export demand

ARAD.description: Aradei Capital — Morocco's first listed REIT (Real Estate Investment Trust), focused on commercial real estate. Portfolio of shopping centers and retail parks anchored by Carrefour/Label'Vie hypermarkets. Backed by Ynna Holding. Yield-oriented stock paying regular dividends.
risks: cap rate expansion compressing asset values

macro_sensitivity.rate_hike: negative — raises cap rates, compresses REIT asset valuations; also raises cost of acquisition debt
macro_sensitivity.gdp_growth: positive — higher consumer spending drives retail tenant revenues and lease renewals
macro_sensitivity.inflation: mixed — CPI-linked rent escalation clauses are positive; input cost inflation raises maintenance costs
macro_sensitivity.e_commerce_growth: negative long-term — reduces brick-and-mortar retail traffic

SOT.description: Sothema — leading Moroccan pharmaceutical manufacturer producing generic and branded drugs, oncology treatments, and hospital medicines. Supplies domestic market and exports to Africa and francophone markets.
key_drivers: Morocco's AMO universal health coverage rollout
R&D pipeline and new product launches
risks: drug price regulatory caps (government sets pharmaceutical prices)
API supply chain disruption (mostly China/India sourced)

macro_sensitivity.amo_expansion: positive — more insured patients means higher reimbursed drug volumes
macro_sensitivity.dirham_weakness: negative — APIs imported in EUR/USD become more expensive
macro_sensitivity.china_india_supply: high risk — API supply disruption (as seen in COVID-19) raises input costs sharply
macro_sensitivity.gdp_growth: moderate positive — higher incomes drive OTC drug spending

COL.description: Colorado — Morocco's leading paint and coatings manufacturer. Products sold under the Colorado brand for interior/exterior decoration, industrial coatings, and waterproofing. Revenue closely tied to construction and renovation activity.
key_drivers: construction and renovation activity in Morocco
raw material costs (titanium dioxide, resins, solvents)
risks: input cost inflation (titanium dioxide, petrochemical derivatives)

macro_sensitivity.gdp_growth: positive — construction activity drives paint demand
macro_sensitivity.oil_rise: negative — most paint raw materials are petrochemical derivatives
macro_sensitivity.government_spend: positive — public infrastructure projects and school/hospital renovations drive large orders
macro_sensitivity.rate_hike: negative — slows housing starts and renovation activity

DARI.description: Dari Couspate — Morocco's leading pasta and couscous manufacturer. Products include couscous, pasta (vermicelli, spaghetti), and flour-based staples. Sold domestically and exported to Europe and Africa.
key_drivers: export volumes to diaspora and African markets
risks: durum wheat price spikes (drought in producer regions)
government price regulation on staple foods

macro_sensitivity.durum_wheat_rise: negative — raises input costs; government caps may prevent full pass-through to consumers
macro_sensitivity.drought_north_africa: negative — reduces domestic wheat supply, raises import cost
macro_sensitivity.war_ukraine: negative — Ukraine is a major durum wheat exporter; Black Sea conflict drives prices up
macro_sensitivity.gdp_growth: low — pasta/couscous are staple foods, demand is inelastic
macro_sensitivity.diaspora_demand: positive — Moroccan diaspora in Europe (France, Spain, Italy) drives steady export volumes

BRM.description: Brasseries du Maroc — Morocco's only beer producer and a Heineken affiliate. Brands include Casablanca, Flag, and licensed Heineken. Monopoly position in the domestic beer and soft drink market. Consistent dividend payer with stable cash flows.
risks: regulatory risk — government alcohol taxation or sale restrictions
limited growth ceiling in majority-Muslim market

macro_sensitivity.tourism_rise: positive — tourist arrivals and hotel/restaurant trade drive beer volumes
macro_sensitivity.gdp_growth: mild positive — beer is affordable; not strongly correlated
macro_sensitivity.barley_price_rise: negative — main brewing input; no domestic substitute
macro_sensitivity.regulation_tightening: high risk — any change to alcohol licensing, taxation, or sale hours is a direct revenue impact
macro_sensitivity.eur_mad: low sensitivity — primarily domestic MAD revenues

LES.description: Lesieur Cristal — Morocco's leading cooking oil and margarine manufacturer, majority owned by the French Avril group. Brands include Lesieur, Cristal, and Huilor. Dominant shelf presence in Moroccan supermarkets and traditional trade.
key_drivers: palm oil and sunflower oil world prices (main raw materials)
government subsidy on cooking oil (if applicable)
risks: palm oil price volatility — margins are highly sensitive to input costs
government price caps on edible oils limiting pass-through
currency risk on USD-denominated palm oil imports

macro_sensitivity.palm_oil_rise: negative — main input cost; compresses margins sharply when world prices spike
macro_sensitivity.palm_oil_fall: positive — margin expansion; historically the strongest profit driver
macro_sensitivity.dirham_weakness: negative — palm oil is priced in USD; weaker MAD raises import cost
macro_sensitivity.gdp_growth: low — cooking oil is a staple; inelastic demand
macro_sensitivity.subsidy_removal: mixed — removal raises consumer prices but may unlock better margins if previously capped

CDN.description: Centrale Danone — Danone's Moroccan subsidiary producing dairy products (milk, yogurt, fresh dairy). One of Morocco's most recognized food brands. Was severely impacted by a social media-driven consumer boycott in 2018 but has been recovering. Danone group holds majority stake.
key_drivers: milk collection volumes from Moroccan farmers
risks: Danone group may sell or restructure Morocco subsidiary
competition from Copag and private-label dairy

macro_sensitivity.raw_milk_rise: negative — raises production cost; hard to fully pass through to price-sensitive consumers
macro_sensitivity.gdp_growth: positive — higher incomes shift consumption toward branded dairy
macro_sensitivity.danone_strategy: high impact — any global Danone restructuring announcement directly affects CDN valuation
macro_sensitivity.drought: negative — reduces domestic milk collection, forcing costlier sourcing

NXM.description: Nexans Maroc — Moroccan subsidiary of the French Nexans group, manufacturing electric cables and wires for energy, construction, and industrial applications. Positioned to benefit from Morocco's renewable energy buildout and World Cup 2030 infrastructure.
key_drivers: Morocco renewable energy infrastructure capex (solar, wind farms)
automotive wire harness exports to Europe
risks: copper price spikes raise input costs before customer price adjustments

macro_sensitivity.copper_rise: negative short-term — main raw material; margins compress before prices adjust
macro_sensitivity.morocco_renewables_capex: strongly positive — solar and wind projects require kilometers of high-voltage cable
macro_sensitivity.world_cup_2030: positive — stadium, rail, and hotel electrical infrastructure is a direct demand driver
macro_sensitivity.automotive_exports: positive — Morocco's growing auto manufacturing hub (Renault, Stellantis) drives wire harness demand

DSW.description: Disway — Morocco's leading IT products distributor, supplying HP, Lenovo, Dell, and other brands to retailers and corporate clients across Morocco and West Africa. Benefits from Morocco's digital transformation push and growing enterprise IT spending.
key_drivers: EUR/MAD and USD/MAD (products priced in foreign currency)
risks: margin compression from vendor direct sales
economic slowdown reducing discretionary IT investment

macro_sensitivity.gdp_growth: positive — IT spending correlates with corporate investment confidence
macro_sensitivity.dirham_weakness: negative — IT products priced in USD/EUR become more expensive in MAD, compressing margins
macro_sensitivity.government_digitalization: positive — public sector IT procurement programs are a direct revenue driver
macro_sensitivity.world_cup_2030: positive — venue, hotel, and transport IT infrastructure procurement

ALM.description: Aluminium du Maroc — manufacturer of aluminum profiles and products for the construction, automotive, and industrial sectors. Positioned to benefit from Morocco's industrial growth as an automotive and aerospace manufacturing hub.
key_drivers: automotive sector growth (Renault, Stellantis Morocco plants)
risks: competition from cheap Chinese aluminum imports
automotive sector slowdown affecting OEM demand

macro_sensitivity.aluminum_rise: positive — direct revenue uplift on product pricing
macro_sensitivity.electricity_cost: high sensitivity — aluminum production is extremely energy-intensive
macro_sensitivity.morocco_auto_hub: positive — Renault and Stellantis Morocco production expansion drives local aluminum demand
macro_sensitivity.china_dumping: negative — Chinese overproduction depresses global aluminum prices

CSA.description: Cartier Saada — Moroccan olive oil producer and agri-food company based in the Souss-Massa region, one of Morocco's most productive olive growing areas. Exports olive oil to the EU, USA, and Middle East. Positioned to capture market share as European olive oil production declines.
key_drivers: olive harvest quality in Morocco (rainfall dependent)
global olive oil prices (driven by Spanish/Italian production)
organic and premium olive oil premiums
risks: harvest variability — drought or frost devastates olive yields
competition from Spanish and Greek producers when their harvests recover

macro_sensitivity.europe_olive_oil_shortage: strongly positive — when Spanish/Italian crops fail (drought, XYLELLA disease), global prices spike and Moroccan exporters gain market share
macro_sensitivity.morocco_drought: negative — dry years in Souss-Massa reduce harvest volumes
macro_sensitivity.eur_usd_strength: positive — export revenues in EUR/USD convert to more MAD
macro_sensitivity.health_trend: positive — global shift toward Mediterranean diet drives premium olive oil demand in Europe and North America
